title: Cold Ashby V West Haddon
content:
A fantastic afternoon of bowls was enjoyed by all on a beautifully prepared home green.

The match was tightly contested early on, but our triples clicked into gear during the second half. Dean and Roger picked up crucial shots on the 14th and 15th ends to pull us ahead.

Thank you to everyone who came out to support. Next week we look forward to our away fixture.

title: Cold Ashby V Rothwell Manor
content:
What a difference a few months makes. After the disappointment of the reverse fixture earlier in the season which fell victim to the unpredictable British weather and had to be cancelled entirely, the return match at Cold Ashby was played in absolutely glorious conditions.

It proved to be an incredibly close and competitive afternoon of bowls, with both teams finding their rhythm on a fine green. Ultimately, Cold Ashby's strength across the rinks secured the day, with the home side taking the victory on two rinks, while the visitors claimed one.

When the final wood was delivered and the scores were tallied, Cold Ashby emerged victorious with a hard-earned 45 to 38 win.

A special mention goes to the afternoon's standout performance for the home side: the formidable trio of Rick, Dean, and Harry, who put on a superb display to finish as Cold Ashby's top winning rink.

Thank you to Rothwell Manor for a fantastic game played in great spirit, and well done to everyone involved.

title: Carlsberg V Cold Ashby
content:
Cold Ashby Secure Solid Away Win in Historical Northampton Clash.

Cold Ashby Bowls Club braved the pre-match elements to secure a convincing 46-35 shot victory against Carlsberg, played at their historic Northampton venue.

title: West Haddon V Cold Ashby
content: On Thursday we travelled the short distance to play at West Haddon. On a lovely summer evening all players enjoyed the match. Cold Ashby were in control for all the match, winning convincingly.

title: Local Derby Delight as Cold Ashby Dominate South Kilworth
content: Cold Ashby made the short six-mile trip to near-neighbours South Kilworth this weekend, returning home with a convincing and hard-fought victory. It was a dominant performance from our team.

title: Desborough V Cold Ashby
content:
Final Score: Desborough 71 – 47 Cold Ashby

It was a challenging afternoon on the road for Cold Ashby, as the visitors struggled to come to terms with some very difficult playing conditions.

title: Cold Ashby v Geddington
content: Unfortunately this match was called off due to the weather.

title: Rothwell Manor V Cold Ashby
content: Unfortunately this match was called off due to the weather.

title: Bugbrooke v Cold Ashby
content:
Cold Ashby endured a tough afternoon on the road, facing not only a strong opponent but also punishingly hot and sultry conditions.

The elements played a major factor in the contest.

title: Club Competitions & BBQ
content:
Our scheduled opponents unfortunately had to cancel as they were unable to field a team.

However, the blank Saturday did not go to waste. The afternoon was instead used to advantage for club competitions and a BBQ gathering.

title: Cold Ashby V Rugby Railway
content: On a bitterly cold evening, Cold Ashby entertained one of the strongest teams in the area. Despite the drop in temperature, the home side put up a gallant fight from the opening ends.

title: Whyte Melville V Cold Ashby
content: Cold Ashby traveled to Fernie Fields on Sunday—the home of Whyte Melville Bowls Club—for a closely contested match. Despite a forecast threatening rain, the weather held out.

title: Harpole V Cold Ashby
content: Cold Ashby traveled to Harpole on Saturday for a closely contested encounter. While the final scoreline suggests a relatively tight margin of 16 shots, the narrative of the match was dramatic.

title: North Kilworth vs Cold Ashby
content: In a challenging away fixture, Cold Ashby fell short against a disciplined North Kilworth side. The story of the day was the heavy rink at North Kilworth requiring significant weight adjustments.

title: Daventry V Cold Ashby
content:
That is a fantastic turnaround! Winning by 9 shots overall (84 to 75) shows a real team effort. A huge shout-out to Chris H, Harry, and Damo for leading the charge as the winning rink.

Match Highlights:
• The Comeback: Successfully snapped a two-game losing streak.
• Final Score: Cold Ashby 84 | Daventry 75
• Standout Performance: Chris, Harry, and Damo winning their rink.

title: Cold Ashby V L.V.A.
content:
Under a cloudless sky, Cold Ashby entertained L.V.A. in what proved to be a highly competitive afternoon. The home team ultimately lost narrowly by 56 shots to 51.

The standout performance came from the winning rink of Rick, Harry, and guest player Pete Ridley.

title: Club Competition Report: The Triples Cup
content:
Overview:
The season kicked off in spectacular fashion as the club hosted its first official competition of the year: The Triples Cup. Players were treated to ideal conditions, competing under a clear blue sky that set the stage for a day of high-caliber play and spirited camaraderie.

The Match:
The tournament lived up to the anticipation, proving to be an exceptionally tight contest. Momentum shifted several times throughout the afternoon, with neither side able to pull away significantly. The battle for the trophy came down to the final ends, characterized by precision shots and strategic positioning. In the end, the narrowest of margins decided the day, with only 3 shots separating the winners from the runners-up.

Results:
Congratulations to our podium finishers who displayed fantastic teamwork and skill:

Winning Team Members: Roger, Dean, & Pedro
Runners-up: Ollie, Matt, & Harry

Note: A huge thank you to everyone who participated and helped make the first competition of the season such a success. We look forward to seeing this level of competition continue throughout the year!

title: Season Opener: Cold Ashby V Fleckney
content:
The bowls season kicked off under a clear sky for the opening match. While it was a fine but cool day, the crisp air didn't dampen the spirits of the players, and the green was noted to be in excellent condition, providing a true surface for the first competitive rolls of the year.

The Result:
It was a nail-biting encounter that went down to the final woods. In a remarkably close contest, Cold Ashby lost narrowly by just one shot, with a final score of:
Fleckney: 71 shots
Cold Ashby: 70 shots

Rink Highlights:
The overall scoreline reflected a day of contrasting fortunes across the rinks.

The Standouts:
The home team's top-performing rink featured Chris, Stuart, and Damo, who secured a solid win for the club.

The Struggle:
It was a tough afternoon for one home rink in particular, which suffered a heavy defeat of 26 shots to 6. This 20-shot deficit proved difficult to overcome, despite the strong performances elsewhere on the green.

Summary:
Despite the heartbreaking one-shot loss, the quality of the green and the competitive nature of the game suggest a promising season ahead for Cold Ashby. With a bit more consistency across all rinks, that single-shot margin will surely swing in their favor next time.

title: Meet and Greet
content:
Members of Cold Ashby Bowls Club held their pre-season get together on April 11th, 2026. There was an excellent turnout, with lots of interest shown from potential new members.

The event included the first roll up on the green, which is in superb condition. The first bowl of the season was bowled by Robert Carpenter.

title: Green Maintenance
content: A huge thanks to Roger Miller and the grounds team for their tireless work over the winter. The green is looking fantastic and ready for a busy 2026 season.
